data.title: Walgreens Boots Alliance beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; withdraws outlook
data.description:
• Walgreens Boots Alliance press release (NASDAQ:WBA): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.63 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $38.6B (+4.0% Y/Y) beats by $700M.
  • Net cash used for operating activities was $199 million in the second quarter, a $438 million improvement compared with the year-ago quarter.
  • Free cash flow2 was negative $418 million, a $192 million improvement compared with the year-ago quarter.

  • Fiscal 2025 guidance

    • Given the pending transaction, pursuant to which WBA will be acquired by entities affiliated with Sycamore Partners, the company is withdrawing fiscal 2025 guidance.

data.title: Tilray reports mixed Q3 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Tilray press release (NASDAQ:TLRY): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.00 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $185.8M (-1.3% Y/Y) misses by $23.97M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA in the third quarter was $9.0 million compared to $10.2 million in the prior year quarter.
  • Beverage alcohol net revenue increased to $55.9 million in the third quarter up from $54.7 million in the prior year quarter.
  • Cannabis net revenue was $54.3 million in the third quarter compared to $63.4 million in the prior year quarter. 
  • Distribution net revenue increased 8% to $61.5 million in the third quarter compared to $56.8 million in the prior year quarter.
  • Wellness net revenue increased 5% to $14.1 million and 8% on a constant currency basis to $14.5 million in the third quarter compared to $13.4 million in the prior year quarter.

  • Company’s Fiscal Year 2025 Guidance

    The Company revises fiscal year 2025 guidance for net revenue to $850 million to $900 million vs. $891.94M consensus. Adjustments for constant currency and the impacts of the strategic initiatives and SKU rationalization, which total approximately $50 million, would have resulted in expected net revenue of $900 million to $950 million.

data.title: El Pollo Loco rallies after receiving a buyout offer from Biglari Capital
data.description:
El Pollo Loco Holdings (NASDAQ:LOCO) disclosed late on Monday that it received an unsolicited, non-binding indication of interest from Biglari Capital Corp. to acquire the rest of the company that it does not already own. Biglari holds through various entities about 4.5 million shares of El Pollo Loco, or about 15.1% of the company.

The El Pollo Loco (NASDAQ:LOCO) board of directors is in the process of carefully evaluating the proposal in consultation with its independent financial and legal advisors to determine the course of action it believes is in the best interests of the company and shareholders. In connection with the board’s evaluation of the proposal, the restaurant operator and Biglari Capital entered into a confidentiality agreement that contains a customary standstill.

The El Pollo Loco (LOCO) board has not set a timetable for any further actions regarding Biglari Capital’s indication of interest at this time. The company does not intend to provide further comment on Biglari Capital’s indication of interest or on a potential transaction with Biglari Capital until it determines that additional disclosure is appropriate or necessary. 

Biglari Capital and Biglari Holdings own or have stakes in Steak 'n Shake, Western Sizzlin, MAxim Magazine, and Southern Oil Company, Cracker Barrel Old Country Store (CBRL), and Jack in the Box (JACK). 

Shares of El Pollo Loco (LOCO) jumped 10.9% in premarket trading to $10.29 vs. the 52-week range of $8.91 to $9.83.

data.title: RPM misses Q3 top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q4 outlook
data.description:
• RPM press release (NYSE:RPM): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.35 misses by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $1.48B (-2.6% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Adjusted EBIT of $78.2 million.
  • Third-quarter cash provided by operating activities of $91.5 million, second highest third-quarter amount in company history.
  • Fiscal 2025 fourth-quarter outlook calls for flat sales and adjusted EBIT to be up in the low-single-digit percentage range.

  • The company expects the following in the fiscal 2025 fourth quarter:

    • Consolidated sales to be flat compared to prior-year results.
    • CPG sales to be flat compared to prior-year record results.
    • PCG sales to increase in the mid-single-digit percentage range compared to prior-year results.
    • SPG sales to decline in the low-single-digit percentage range compared to prior-year results.
    • Consumer Group sales to decline in the low-single-digit percentage range compared to prior-year results.
    • Consolidated adjusted EBIT to be up in the low-single-digit percentage range compared to prior-year record results.

data.title: Air Lease provides Q1 update
data.description:
• Air Lease (NYSE:AL) said at the end of March that its fleet included 487 owned aircraft and 57 managed aircraft, with 255 new aircraft on order from Airbus and Boeing set to deliver through 2029.
  • The firm said it delivered 14 new aircraft from AL’s orderbook including four Airbus A220s, eight Boeing 737-8s, one Boeing 737-9, and one Boeing 787-10.
  • The company sold 16 aircraft to third-party buyers, with sales proceeds for the quarter totaling ~$520 million.

data.title: Arco Dorados updates on its strategies to optimize sales growth
data.description:
Arcos Dorado (NYSE:ARCO) issued a strategy update as part of the Argentina-based company's annual letter to shareholders.

The letter detailed plans to roll out more features in the restaurant operator's mobile app and implement a loyalty program in all markets in 2025. Arcos Dorados (NYSE:ARCO) said that it believes the company has just begun to scratch the surface in its ability to drive guest frequency and lifetime value. 

McDonald's in Latin America and the Caribbean was noted to offer an unmatched array of restaurant formats, sales channels, menu options and service models. Arcos Dorados (ARCO) thinks those formats can be managed and adjusted to optimize sales growth and profitability, consistently over time.

"The industry has permanently changed since 2020, and Arcos Dorados has positioned the McDonald's (MCD) brand to provide QSR customers the most complete experience and best value in Latin America and the Caribbean," highlighted the company."

"We operate the most modernized restaurant portfolio in the region, with EOTF restaurants making up 67% of the total footprint at year-end 2024. Importantly, we are working to reach at least 90% EOTF penetration by year-end 2027. We are also operating from a position of strength in Latin America, with significant market share leadership and an industry-benchmark Digital platform."

"Our long-term, supportive partnership with McDonald's is an important competitive strength. We share a vision for the future of the Brand in Latin America and the Caribbean, and we are aligned strategically in terms of how to best realize that vision for at least the next couple of decades."

Shares of Arcos Dorados Holdings (ARCO) rose 3.9% in premarket trading to $7.28.

data.title: N2OFF plans to invest an additional €25,000 in Germany
data.description:
N2OFF (NASDAQ:NITO) Tuesday unveiled potential plans to invest an additional €25,000, in addition to prior loans made to Solterra Renewable Energy to pursue the development of a new Battery Energy Storage System project to be co-located with its 111 MWp solar power plant in Melz, Germany.

This initiative follows the approval of a new German regulation that enables project owners to connect additional assets, such as battery storage, to the same grid connection as long as the approved capacity is not exceeded.

The move aims to maximize the use of limited grid infrastructure and accelerate the deployment of renewable energy projects.

data.title: Wall Street's fear gauge falls as hopes for trade negotiations rise
data.description:
Wall Street's volatility index on Tuesday dropped further away from 50 points amid a broader rebound in equities as hopes grew that the U.S. might enter into trade negotiations with some countries.

U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday his administration won't pump the brakes on imposing tariffs on products coming into the U.S., but he’s open to discussing trade deals with other countries.

The Cboe Volatility Index (VIX) fell -8.5% to 42.97 points. The VIX (VIX) had briefly topped 50 points on Thursday. 

The CNN Fear & Greed Index, however, slipped further into the Extreme Fear territory.

"Admittedly, Trump did sound open to reaching deals with other countries, saying in his message on China tariffs that 'Negotiations with other countries, which have also requested meetings, will begin taking place immediately,'" Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen added.

The sentiment across markets was broadly better on Tuesday, with U.S. stock index futures higher;  Asia-Pacific stocks also rebounded, and European markets recovered on hopes that U.S. may soften tariff stance

data.title: Titan Mining gets new finance chief
data.description:
• Titan Mining (OTCQB:TIMCF) appointed Mr. Kevin Hart as chief financial officer, effective immediately.
  • Kevin Hart is an executive with over two decades of experience in mineral exploration, development, construction and operations, with a focus on both base and precious metals. 
  • Most recently, Kevin served as the CFO and Corporate Secretary for Inca One Gold Corp.

data.title: RFK Jr. wants CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
data.description:
Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is moving ahead with his plan to scrutinize fluoride in public drinking water, and plans to tell the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to stop recommending fluoridation.

The Environmental Protection Agency will review scientific information on the potential health risks of fluoridation. The Department of Health and Human Services is reconvening the Community Preventive Services Task Force to study and make a new recommendation on fluoride.

The reviews at the federal level follow Utah becoming the first U.S. state to ban adding fluoride, which has been proven to help reduce cavities, in public drinking water.

"Almost all water contains some naturally occurring fluoride, but usually at levels too low to prevent cavities," according to the CDC. It has said community water fluoridation is a "practical, cost-effective, and equitable way" to improve residents' oral health, and listed drinking water fluoridation as one of the 10 great public health interventions of the 20th century.

But RFK Jr. previously claimed that fluoride is "an industrial waste associated with arthritis, bone fractures, bone cancer, IQ loss, neurodevelopmental disorders, and thyroid disease."

The National Toxicology Program in 2024 concluded "with moderate confidence" that higher levels of fluoride exposure (more than 1.5 milligrams of fluoride per liter of water) are associated with lower IQ in children.

But it noted that there was insufficient data to determine if the 0.7 mg/L level currently recommended for U.S. community water has a negative effect on children's IQ. "The NTP found no evidence that fluoride exposure had adverse effects on adult cognition."
